subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse the characteristics of natural wonders and their importance
Understand the ecological significance of the Valley of Flowers
Examine the unique construction and sustainability of Living Root Bridges
Develop critical thinking about the relationship between humans and nature
Strengthen reading comprehension and descriptive vocabulary
Encourage awareness about conservation of natural heritage

lesson_plan.title: Exploring Natural Wonders
topics_covered: Difference between natural and man-made wonders
student_activities: Classify examples into natural and man-made wonders
Create a small concept map about nature’s power

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Valley of Flowers
topics_covered: Variety of flowers and plant species
student_activities: List plant and animal species mentioned in the chapter

lesson_plan.title: Importance of Biodiversity
topics_covered: Role of ecosystems in maintaining balance
student_activities: Write a short explanation of why natural habitats should be protected

lesson_plan.title: Living Root Bridges – A Natural Engineering Wonder
student_activities: Explain the process of creating root bridges
Compare natural bridges with man-made bridges

lesson_plan.title: Human Responsibility Towards Nature
topics_covered: Role of communities in protecting nature
student_activities: Suggest ways to protect natural wonders
Prepare a short awareness message about conservation

teaching_aids: Images or slides of the Valley of Flowers and Living Root Bridges
teacher_tip: Encourage students to compare natural wonders with man-made structures to understand how nature and human creativity both contribute to spectacular creations.
